descriptions.monitors_delete: Permanently delete a monitor by id. Required: id (path)  -  the monitor's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This is irreversible and stops all checks, alerting, and SLA tracking for that monitor; its historical incidents are no longer associated with a live monitor. Prefer monitors_update with paused=true if you only want to silence a monitor temporarily rather than remove it.
descriptions.monitor-groups_delete: Permanently delete a monitor group by id. Required: id (path)  -  the monitor group's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This removes the group container only; member monitors are ungrouped, not deleted. Use monitors_delete to remove the underlying monitors. Irreversible.
descriptions.heartbeats_delete: Permanently delete a heartbeat (cron/scheduled-task check-in) by id. Required: id (path)  -  the heartbeat's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This is irreversible and stops expected-check-in monitoring and missed-heartbeat alerting for that job. Prefer heartbeats_update with paused=true to silence a heartbeat without losing its configuration.
descriptions.heartbeat-groups_delete: Permanently delete a heartbeat group by id. Required: id (path)  -  the heartbeat group's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This removes the group container only; member heartbeats are ungrouped, not deleted. Use heartbeats_delete to remove the underlying heartbeats. Irreversible.
descriptions.incidents_delete: Permanently delete an incident by id. Required: id (path)  -  the incident's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This is irreversible and erases the incident from history, which affects MTTA/MTTR and on-call reporting. Prefer incidents_resolve (the incidents resolve action) to close an active incident while keeping its record; only delete to remove a test or erroneously created incident.
descriptions.policies_delete: Permanently delete an escalation policy by id. Required: id (path)  -  the policy's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This is irreversible; monitors or heartbeats still referencing the deleted policy lose that escalation path, so re-point them to another policy first to avoid silent alerting gaps.
descriptions.status-pages_delete: Permanently delete a status page by id. Required: id (path)  -  the status page's Better Stack id. Returns HTTP 204 with no body on success. This is irreversible and takes the public status page offline along with its sections (status-page-sections_*) and resource subscriptions. There is no soft-disable; only delete when you intend to retire the page entirely.
